events.event_label: Bta03000 and Bta02903 encode hsp70
events.evidence_context_text: PMC8909865.p053.s015: Significantly increased transmission efficiency of CLCuV was noted in B. tabaci Asia II 1 when hsp70 was silenced. PMC8909865.p053.s016: The difference may be due to the variation in virus species and B. tabaci cryptic species. PMC8909865.p053.s017: The interactions of B. tabaci with begomoviruses are not conserved and alter with cryptic species of B. tabaci and begomovirus species. PMC8909865.p053.s018: B. tabaci MEAM1 has two genes (Bta03000 and Bta02903) annotated as hsp70, and their amino acid sequences differ by 97.55%. PMC8909865.p053.s019: They might also exhibit differential responses to begomovirus infection.

events.event_label: hsp70 dsRNA reduces B. tabaci survival and ChiLCV transmission
events.evidence_context_text: PMC8909865.p054.s003: The naked dsRNA quickly degrades in higher temperatures and UV radiation under natural environmental conditions. PMC8909865.p054.s004: Besides, several insect species, mainly lepidopteran insects, have been observed to be refractory to RNAi due to degradation and poor intracellular transport of exogenous dsRNA. PMC8909865.p054.s005: Nucleases are the major factors limiting the efficacy of dsRNA. PMC8909865.p054.s006: In the first phase of the study, hsp70 dsRNA showed high potential in eradicating B. tabaci and inhibiting ChiLCV transmission. PMC8909865.p054.s007: In the next phase, the dsRNA construct was assessed under natural environmental conditions by spraying like an insecticide.

events.event_label: hsp70 dsRNA decreases ChiLCV copies and transmission
events.evidence_context_text: PMC8909865.p054.s012: A 67.77% mortality with a 4.6-fold decrease in hsp70 mRNA levels in B. tabaci exposed to the dsRNA-sprayed plants was noted after 24 h of release. PMC8909865.p054.s013: Further, 1.84 × 108-fold decreased ChiLCV copies and 75% reduced transmission of ChiLCV indicated the efficiency of topically sprayed hsp70 dsRNA in managing both the virus and its vector. PMC8909865.p054.s014: Although the efficacy of topically applied naked dsRNA against plant viruses is known, this is the first evidence of its efficacy against a hemipteran insect like B. tabaci.

events.event_label: hsp70 dsRNA protects plants by affecting B. tabaci and ChiLCV
events.evidence_context_text: PMC8909865.p055.s006: Infection of ChiLCV at the seedling stage leads to a considerable reduction in plant height, internodal length, and fruiting. PMC8909865.p055.s007: Hence, a 20-day shield by 2 consecutive sprays of naked hsp70 dsRNA would protect the crucial phase of crop growth from the invasion of B. tabaci and ChiLCV and reduce yield losses. PMC8909865.p055.s008: The foliar spray of hsp70 dsRNA was effective under semi-field conditions where the daily temperature fluctuated between 20–35 °C with 40–60% RH. PMC8909865.p055.s009: The efficacy and persistency of topically sprayed naked dsRNA at a wide temperature range endorse its potentiality for field uses as a spray-on technology.

events.event_label: DNA-A component detected in chilli plants and matches ChiLCV isolates
events.evidence_context_text: PMC8909865.p035.s001: The identity of the virus was confirmed by sequencing the DNA-A component from infected chilli plants. PMC8909865.p035.s002: PCR amplification of the DNA-A using Begomo F and Begomo R primer produced a 2.7 kb product visualized on 1% agarose gel. PMC8909865.p035.s003: Bidirectional sequencing of the cloned products produced a 2763 nt sequence comprising complete DNA-A that showed 100% nucleotide identity to ChiLCV isolates upon BLASTn analysis. PMC8909865.p035.s004: The sequence can be retrieved by GenBank Accession No. OM513903.

events.event_label: dsRNA treatment reduces transmission, hsp70 expression and increases mortality of B. tabaci
events.evidence_context_text: PMC8909865.p054.s009: Hence, naked hsp70 dsRNA without any delivery agent was topically sprayed onto the foliage of the ChiLCV-infected chilli plant under contained semi-field conditions. PMC8909865.p054.s010: B. tabaci adults were released on the dsRNA-sprayed plants to evaluate the dsRNA’s efficacy in eradicating B. tabaci and inhibiting the transmission of ChiLCV. PMC8909865.p054.s011: Considering the extracellular degradation of dsRNA and delivery through the host plant, a 10-fold higher dose than the controlled assay was used for topical application. PMC8909865.p054.s012: A 67.77% mortality with a 4.6-fold decrease in hsp70 mRNA levels in B. tabaci exposed to the dsRNA-sprayed plants was noted after 24 h of release. PMC8909865.p054.s013: Further, 1.84 × 108-fold decreased ChiLCV copies and 75% reduced transmission of ChiLCV indicated the efficiency of topically sprayed hsp70 dsRNA in managing both the virus and its vector.

events.event_label: spray-on hsp70 dsRNA provides resistance to B. tabaci and ChiLCV
events.evidence_context_text: PMC8909865.p057.s001: In conclusion, the spray-on application of naked dsRNA targeting hsp70 of B. tabaci provides resistance to both ChiLCV and its vector, B. tabaci. PMC8909865.p057.s002: This is a novel alternative to hazardous insecticides and may be further assessed on a large scale for its efficacy under real-field conditions.

events.event_label: Semi-field dsRNA treatment increases mortality and downregulates hsp70
events.evidence_context_text: PMC8909865.p002.s006: However, ChiLCV copies significantly increased in fas2 dsRNA-fed B. tabaci. PMC8909865.p002.s007: Transmission of ChiLCV by B. tabaci was completely inhibited post-24 h feeding on hsp70 dsRNA at 3 μg/mL. Naked hsp70 dsRNA was topically sprayed on ChiLCV-infected chilli plants like an insecticide. PMC8909865.p002.s008: 67.77% mortality of B. tabaci, 4.6-fold downregulation of hsp70 mRNA, and 1.34 × 1015-fold decreased ChiLCV copies in B. tabaci were recorded when adults were exposed to the dsRNA-treated plants under semi-field conditions. PMC8909865.p002.s009: Foliar application of naked dsRNA reduced the ChiLCV transmission by 75% without any visible symptoms in the inoculated plants. PMC8909865.p002.s010: A total of 2 consecutive sprays of dsRNA provided significant protection to B. tabaci for up to 20 days under semi-field conditions.

events.event_label: Knockdown of hsp70 and fas2 confers tolerance to ChiLCV infection
events.evidence_context_text: PMC8909865.p052.s006: The fas2 mRNA expression level was upregulated in viruliferous B. tabaci by 2.6 and 4.518-fold in the RNA-Seq and RT-qPCR assay, respectively. PMC8909865.p052.s007: The Fas2 orthologue in mammals (NCAM) is known to serve as a receptor for the rabies virus. PMC8909865.p052.s008: Hence, we targeted to knock down these two genes, hsp70 and fas2, to induce immunity to ChiLCV infection in B. tabaci. PMC8909865.p052.s009: Conserved 128 and 150 nt stretches in the hsp70 and fas2 genes, respectively, were found to be specific to B. tabaci without any cross-reactivity to humans, mice, birds, butterflies, bees, ants, and plants. PMC8909865.p052.s010: Each produced a putative siRNA of 21 nt.

events.event_label: dsRNA exposure downregulates hsp70 and fas2 expression
events.evidence_context_text: PMC8909865.p052.s018: Similarly, fas2 dsRNA at 3.0 μg/mL caused 72% mortality of B. tabaci 24 h post-feeding. PMC8909865.p052.s019: The dsRNA exposure downregulated the hsp70 and fas2 mRNA expressions by 12.85- and 8.53-fold, respectively. PMC8909865.p052.s020: The expression of an endogenous control gene was unregulated post-dsRNA exposure. PMC8909865.p052.s021: This indicated the specificity of the dsRNA constructs to target genes. PMC8909865.p052.s022: Hsp belongs to the multifunctional molecular chaperone family involved in the aggregation of damaged proteins, transportation, assembly, and disassembly of multi-structured units under stressed conditions.

events.event_label: dsRNA feeding reduces hsp70 abundance and causes mortality
events.evidence_context_text: PMC8909865.p052.s021: This indicated the specificity of the dsRNA constructs to target genes. PMC8909865.p052.s023: The mortality of B. tabaci post-dsRNA feeding may be due to the loss of function via depletion of hsp70 mRNA, which might interrupt the normal biological processes in B. tabaci. events.event_label: hsp70 dsRNA feeding reduces ChiLCV copies and inhibits transmission
events.evidence_context_text: PMC8909865.p053.s006: The resistance to ChiLCV in B. tabaci was enhanced at higher concentrations of hsp70 dsRNA. PMC8909865.p053.s007: ChiLCV copies in B. tabaci were imperceptible when B. tabaci fed on 3 μg/mL hsp70 dsRNA for 24 h. The transmission of ChiLCV from infected to healthy plants by B. tabaci Asia II 1 was completely inhibited at 3 μg/mL hsp70 dsRNA treatment under controlled conditions. PMC8909865.p053.s008: Our findings are consistent with Kanakala et al., who reported the deleterious effect of B. tabaci hsp70 dsRNA on TYLCV transmission. PMC8909865.p053.s009: The TYLCV titer decreased in B. tabaci Middle East Asia Minor 1 (MEAM 1) after feeding on hsp70 dsRNA-expressing tomato plants. PMC8909865.p053.s010: Transmission of TYLCV also dropped by 12% post-silencing of hsp70.

events.event_label: hsp70 and fas2 fragments produce siRNA
events.evidence_context_text: PMC8909865.p037.s001: A conserved 128 nt-long (2416 to 2543 nt) fragment of the hsp70 gene (~2.5 kb) and a 150 nt-long (1085 to 1234 nt) fragment of the fas2 gene (~1.4 kb) of B. tabaci were selected for dsRNA designing (Figure 1a,b). PMC8909865.p037.s002: In siRNA Wizard 3.1, the 128 nt-long B. tabaci hsp70 fragment produced a putative siRNA of 21 nt (5’-GAUCCAUCCAUGCCGUUAAUC-3’). PMC8909865.p037.s003: The 150 nt-long fas2 fragment also yielded one putative siRNA of 21 nt (5’-GGACGGAAGAAGGCAUAAUCA-3’). PMC8909865.p037.s004: The dsRNA sequence was specific to B. tabaci, and no cross-reactivity was recorded with other organisms such as Homo sapiens, Mus musculus, Aves (taxid:8782), Lepidoptera (taxid:7088), Hymenoptera (taxid:7399), Formicidae (taxid:36668), and plants (taxid:3193) in BLAST analysis.

events.event_label: dsRNA treatment increases mortality of B. tabaci
events.evidence_context_text: PMC8909865.p039.s006: Oral delivery of fas2 dsRNA at 1.0 μg/mL exhibited 39.33% mean mortality after 24 h (Figure 2b). PMC8909865.p039.s007: Significantly higher mortality (62 and 72%) of B. tabaci adults was recorded when 2.0 and 3.0 μg/mL fas2 dsRNA was orally delivered to B. tabaci under controlled laboratory conditions. PMC8909865.p039.s008: At all doses, the mortality of B. tabaci post-fas2 dsRNA exposure was significantly lower than the hsp70 dsRNA at p < 0.05. PMC8909865.p039.s009: However, no morpho-deformities were observed in killed B. tabaci adults either with hsp70 or fas2 dsRNA.

events.event_label: hsp70 dsRNA reduces hsp70 mRNA expression
events.evidence_context_text: PMC8909865.p040.s001: RT-qPCR analysis showed that hsp70 dsRNA significantly reduced the hsp70 mRNA expression levels in treated B. tabaci. PMC8909865.p040.s002: Hsp70 mRNA levels in adult B. tabaci were decreased by 5.43-fold with respect to an endogenous control gene, β-actin, post-24 h feeding on hsp70 dsRNA (Figure 2c). PMC8909865.p040.s003: The mRNA expression levels were significantly reduced with an increase in hsp70 dsRNA concentration. PMC8909865.p040.s004: An exposure to hsp70 dsRNA at 2.0 μg/mL for 24 h significantly reduced the hsp70 mRNA expression by 11.32-fold and up to 12.85-fold when fed with 3.0 μg/mL hsp70 dsRNA.

events.event_label: hsp70 dsRNA reduces virus copy number
events.evidence_context_text: PMC8909865.p042.s003: The virus copies acquired by the B. tabaci were quantified by fitting the mean CT values obtained in qPCR into the standard curve. PMC8909865.p042.s004: The specificity of the reactions was confirmed by a single peak at 81 °C in the melting curve analysis of qPCR (Figure 3b). PMC8909865.p042.s005: The virus copy number acquired by hsp70 dsRNA-treated B. tabaci was significantly less than the untreated control. PMC8909865.p042.s006: When B. tabaci was fed with sterile distilled water instead of hsp70 dsRNA, the mean ChiLCV copy number was 2.22 × 1015, whereas it decreased by 35.18-fold (6.31 × 1013 copies) post-exposure to hsp70 dsRNA at 1.0 μg/mL (Figure 3c). PMC8909865.p042.s007: The virus copy number in treated B. tabaci further decreased with a gradual increase in hsp70 dsRNA concentration.

events.event_label: dsRNA treatment causes mortality and reduces hsp70 expression
events.evidence_context_text: PMC8909865.p002.s001: Chilli leaf curl virus (ChiLCV; genus: Begomovirus), transmitted by Bemisia tabaci (Gennadius) (Hemiptera: Aleyrodidae) in a persistent-circulative manner, is a major constraint in chilli production. PMC8909865.p002.s002: The present study demonstrates for the first time that a topical spray of naked double-stranded RNA (dsRNA) on chilli plants causes mortality and inability to acquire and transmit ChiLCV in B. tabaci. PMC8909865.p002.s003: dsRNA targeting heat shock protein 70 (hsp70) and fasciclin 2 (fas2) of B. tabaci Asia II 1 was first assessed under controlled conditions through oral delivery. PMC8909865.p002.s004: Hsp70 and fas2 dsRNA resulted in up to 82.22% and 72% mortality of B. tabaci and around 12.4- and 8.5-fold decreases in mRNA levels, respectively, 24 h post-ingestion.

events.event_label: dsRNA feeding reduces hsp70 and fas2 mRNA expression
events.evidence_context_text: PMC8909865.p040.s003: The mRNA expression levels were significantly reduced with an increase in hsp70 dsRNA concentration. PMC8909865.p040.s004: An exposure to hsp70 dsRNA at 2.0 μg/mL for 24 h significantly reduced the hsp70 mRNA expression by 11.32-fold and up to 12.85-fold when fed with 3.0 μg/mL hsp70 dsRNA. PMC8909865.p040.s005: The fas2 mRNA expression levels were declined by 5.48-fold post-24 h feeding on fas2 dsRNA at 1.0 μg/mL. The mRNA expression levels post-hsp70 and -fas2 dsRNA feeding were almost similar at 1.0 μg/mL. The mRNA expression levels were significantly reduced up to 8.53-fold with an increase in fas2 dsRNA concentration. PMC8909865.p040.s006: The reduction in mRNA expression level was significantly higher post-hsp70 dsRNA feeding compared to fas2 dsRNA at 2.0 and 3.0 μg/mL. The primer pairs for hsp70, fas2, and β-actin did not produce any secondary peaks in the RT-qPCR melting curve analysis that indicated the specificity of the reactions (Figure 2d,e).

events.event_label: NCAM induces Interferon-ß and decreases virus titer
events.evidence_context_text: PMC8909865.p053.s021: The Fas2 orthologue in mammals (NCAM) is known to serve as a receptor for the rabies virus. PMC8909865.p053.s022: Although NCAM promotes the penetration of the virus in cells, it suppresses virus replication via induction of Interferon-ß, which is mainly involved in innate immunity against viral infection. PMC8909865.p053.s023: Upregulation of fas2 transcripts in B. tabaci post-ChiLCV infection might be due to the innate immune response against the virus infection. PMC8909865.p053.s024: We hypothesized that fas2 has a negative regulatory role in ChiLCV infection, and thus, knocking down fas2 increased the virus titer in B. tabaci. PMC8909865.p053.s025: Hence, silencing fas2 was not considered to induce resistance to ChiLCV despite its efficacy in eradicating B. tabaci.

events.event_label: dsRNA treatment increases mortality of B. tabaci
events.evidence_context_text: PMC8909865.p002.s001: Chilli leaf curl virus (ChiLCV; genus: Begomovirus), transmitted by Bemisia tabaci (Gennadius) (Hemiptera: Aleyrodidae) in a persistent-circulative manner, is a major constraint in chilli production. PMC8909865.p002.s002: The present study demonstrates for the first time that a topical spray of naked double-stranded RNA (dsRNA) on chilli plants causes mortality and inability to acquire and transmit ChiLCV in B. tabaci. PMC8909865.p002.s003: dsRNA targeting heat shock protein 70 (hsp70) and fasciclin 2 (fas2) of B. tabaci Asia II 1 was first assessed under controlled conditions through oral delivery. PMC8909865.p002.s004: Hsp70 and fas2 dsRNA resulted in up to 82.22% and 72% mortality of B. tabaci and around 12.4- and 8.5-fold decreases in mRNA levels, respectively, 24 h post-ingestion. PMC8909865.p002.s006: However, ChiLCV copies significantly increased in fas2 dsRNA-fed B. tabaci. PMC8909865.p002.s007: Transmission of ChiLCV by B. tabaci was completely inhibited post-24 h feeding on hsp70 dsRNA at 3 μg/mL. Naked hsp70 dsRNA was topically sprayed on ChiLCV-infected chilli plants like an insecticide. PMC8909865.p002.s008: 67.77% mortality of B. tabaci, 4.6-fold downregulation of hsp70 mRNA, and 1.34 × 1015-fold decreased ChiLCV copies in B. tabaci were recorded when adults were exposed to the dsRNA-treated plants under semi-field conditions. PMC8909865.p002.s009: Foliar application of naked dsRNA reduced the ChiLCV transmission by 75% without any visible symptoms in the inoculated plants. PMC8909865.p002.s010: A total of 2 consecutive sprays of dsRNA provided significant protection to B. tabaci for up to 20 days under semi-field conditions.

events.event_label: hsp70 dsRNA reduces ChiLCV transmission
events.evidence_context_text: PMC8909865.p043.s001: A similar pattern was recorded in the transmission of ChiLCV from infected to healthy chilli plants by hsp70 dsRNA-fed B. tabaci. PMC8909865.p043.s002: A 100% transmission of ChiLCV was recorded when B. tabaci was not exposed to hsp70 dsRNA in the control set. PMC8909865.p043.s003: All the inoculated plants produced strong leaf curl and vein-clearing symptoms and tested ChiLCV positive in PCR. PMC8909865.p043.s004: Although there was a significant decrease in ChiLCV copies in B. tabaci exposed to 1.0 μg/mL hsp70 dsRNA, all the test plants were found to be infected with ChiLCV in PCR testing at 35 days post-inoculation. PMC8909865.p043.s006: However, the severity of the leaf curl symptoms in inoculated plants was less than the untreated control. PMC8909865.p043.s007: The transmission of ChiLCV was decreased to 40% when B. tabaci were fed with 2.0 μg/mL hsp70 dsRNA for 24 h (Figure 3e). PMC8909865.p043.s008: The inoculated plants that tested positive in PCR produced mild leaf curl symptoms. PMC8909865.p043.s009: Interestingly, there was no transmission of ChiLCV when B. tabaci was exposed to 3.0 μg/mL hsp70 dsRNA. PMC8909865.p043.s010: All the inoculated plants were without any leaf curl symptoms (Figure 3f) and tested ChiLCV-free in PCR at 35 days post-inoculation.

events.evidence_context_text: PMC8909865.p054.s003: The naked dsRNA quickly degrades in higher temperatures and UV radiation under natural environmental conditions. PMC8909865.p054.s004: Besides, several insect species, mainly lepidopteran insects, have been observed to be refractory to RNAi due to degradation and poor intracellular transport of exogenous dsRNA. PMC8909865.p054.s005: Nucleases are the major factors limiting the efficacy of dsRNA. PMC8909865.p054.s006: In the first phase of the study, hsp70 dsRNA showed high potential in eradicating B. tabaci and inhibiting ChiLCV transmission. PMC8909865.p054.s007: In the next phase, the dsRNA construct was assessed under natural environmental conditions by spraying like an insecticide. PMC8909865.p054.s009: Hence, naked hsp70 dsRNA without any delivery agent was topically sprayed onto the foliage of the ChiLCV-infected chilli plant under contained semi-field conditions. PMC8909865.p054.s010: B. tabaci adults were released on the dsRNA-sprayed plants to evaluate the dsRNA’s efficacy in eradicating B. tabaci and inhibiting the transmission of ChiLCV. PMC8909865.p054.s011: Considering the extracellular degradation of dsRNA and delivery through the host plant, a 10-fold higher dose than the controlled assay was used for topical application. PMC8909865.p054.s012: A 67.77% mortality with a 4.6-fold decrease in hsp70 mRNA levels in B. tabaci exposed to the dsRNA-sprayed plants was noted after 24 h of release. PMC8909865.p054.s013: Further, 1.84 × 108-fold decreased ChiLCV copies and 75% reduced transmission of ChiLCV indicated the efficiency of topically sprayed hsp70 dsRNA in managing both the virus and its vector. PMC8909865.p054.s014: Although the efficacy of topically applied naked dsRNA against plant viruses is known, this is the first evidence of its efficacy against a hemipteran insect like B. tabaci.

events.evidence_context_text: PMC8909865.p051.s006: With the advancements in next-generation sequencing and transcriptomics technology, several genes have been identified as potential targets to control B. tabaci and its viruses. PMC8909865.p005.s010: Control options for ChiLCV and B. tabaci are very limited as insecticides continue to lose their efficacy due to the emergence of insecticide-resistant B. tabaci populations. PMC8909865.p005.s011: Besides, insecticides adversely affect the environment and human health. PMC8909865.p005.s012: Considering the continued lack of consumer acceptance, transgenic plant technology is not a feasible strategy. PMC8909865.p005.s013: In recent years, RNA interference (RNAi) has shown promise in the management of insect pests and plant viruses.

evidence_context_sentences.text: ChiLCV has been responsible for several epidemics in India and Sri Lanka.

The disease is typically manifested in the infected plants as upward curling, puckering, bunching of leaves, blistering of interveinal areas, thickening, and swelling of the veins, shortening of internodes and petioles, and stunting of the whole plant.
The leaves become smaller and severely affected plants produce fewer and deformed fruits.
Yield losses of 20–50% have been recorded in chilli, which may rise to 100% in the case of simultaneous infestation with thrips and mites.

relations.subject: DNA-A component

evidence.text: The identity of the virus was confirmed by sequencing the DNA-A component from infected chilli plants.

relations.evidence_preview: The identity of the virus was confirmed by sequencing the DNA-A component from infected chilli plants.
relations.evidence_context_text: PMC8909865.p035.s001: The identity of the virus was confirmed by sequencing the DNA-A component from infected chilli plants. PMC8909865.p035.s002: PCR amplification of the DNA-A using Begomo F and Begomo R primer produced a 2.7 kb product visualized on 1% agarose gel. PMC8909865.p035.s003: Bidirectional sequencing of the cloned products produced a 2763 nt sequence comprising complete DNA-A that showed 100% nucleotide identity to ChiLCV isolates upon BLASTn analysis. PMC8909865.p035.s004: The sequence can be retrieved by GenBank Accession No. OM513903.

relations.evidence_context_text: PMC8909865.p037.s001: A conserved 128 nt-long (2416 to 2543 nt) fragment of the hsp70 gene (~2.5 kb) and a 150 nt-long (1085 to 1234 nt) fragment of the fas2 gene (~1.4 kb) of B. tabaci were selected for dsRNA designing (Figure 1a,b). PMC8909865.p037.s002: In siRNA Wizard 3.1, the 128 nt-long B. tabaci hsp70 fragment produced a putative siRNA of 21 nt (5’-GAUCCAUCCAUGCCGUUAAUC-3’). PMC8909865.p037.s003: The 150 nt-long fas2 fragment also yielded one putative siRNA of 21 nt (5’-GGACGGAAGAAGGCAUAAUCA-3’). PMC8909865.p037.s004: The dsRNA sequence was specific to B. tabaci, and no cross-reactivity was recorded with other organisms such as Homo sapiens, Mus musculus, Aves (taxid:8782), Lepidoptera (taxid:7088), Hymenoptera (taxid:7399), Formicidae (taxid:36668), and plants (taxid:3193) in BLAST analysis.

relations.subject: insecticides

evidence.text: Several management strategies have been adopted around the world, with insecticides as the core component for the management of the B. tabaci-ChiLCV complex.

However, the use of chemical insecticides has led to several detrimental effects like the development of insecticide-resistant B. tabaci population, destruction of beneficial organisms, environmental contamination, and health hazards.

sentences.text: Topical Spray of dsRNA Induces Mortality and Inhibits Chilli Leaf Curl Virus Transmission by Bemisia tabaci Asia II 1
Chilli leaf curl virus (ChiLCV; genus: Begomovirus), transmitted by Bemisia tabaci (Gennadius) (Hemiptera: Aleyrodidae) in a persistent-circulative manner, is a major constraint in chilli production.
The present study demonstrates for the first time that a topical spray of naked double-stranded RNA (dsRNA) on chilli plants causes mortality and inability to acquire and transmit ChiLCV in B.
tabaci.

The body is yellowish with distinct hyaline wings dusted with white powdery wax.
Adults B.
tabaci can be distinguished from other whiteflies by the position in which the wings are held over the body.
The wings are held close to the body and tent-like in B.
tabaci.
Chilli (Capsicum annum L., family Solanaceae) is one of the economically important crops produced in tropical and sub-tropical countries.
Chilli leaf curl virus (ChiLCV, genus Begomovirus, family Geminiviridae) is a major constraint of chilli production, causing annual losses of about USD 15 billion.
ChiLCV is a monopartite begomovirus that contains one circular, single-stranded DNA-A component of 2.
7 kb in size.
ChiLCV has been responsible for several epidemics in India and Sri Lanka.

The disease is typically manifested in the infected plants as upward curling, puckering, bunching of leaves, blistering of interveinal areas, thickening, and swelling of the veins, shortening of internodes and petioles, and stunting of the whole plant.
The leaves become smaller and severely affected plants produce fewer and deformed fruits.
Yield losses of 20–50% have been recorded in chilli, which may rise to 100% in the case of simultaneous infestation with thrips and mites.
Control options for ChiLCV and B.
The objective of the present study was to induce resistance against B.

In the present study, B.

tabaci hsp70 and fas2 were silenced and resultant effects in mortality and virus transmission were reported. For the first time, the efficacy of spray-on application of naked dsRNA against a hemipteran insect was demonstrated under semi-field conditions.
